exercises.title: Use Redis as a key-value store
exercises.description: Store a key with a TTL, read it back, and watch it expire.
exercises.expected_outcome: The value should be retrievable until the TTL expires.

exercises.title: Compare document shapes
exercises.description: Represent the same data as nested JSON and as flattened relational rows and compare the shape.
exercises.expected_outcome: The document version should keep related fields together in one object.

exercises.title: Build an in-memory document store
exercises.description: Support insert and nested-field lookup for a tiny document collection.
exercises.expected_outcome: Nested fields should be queryable without flattening the whole object.

exercises.title: Simulate hash sharding
exercises.description: Route records across shards by key and inspect how evenly the keys distribute.
exercises.expected_outcome: A good partition key should spread records across shards fairly evenly.

exercises.title: Build a replicated key-value store
exercises.description: Create a primary-replica KV store and observe how writes propagate to followers.
exercises.expected_outcome: Reads from replicas should eventually converge with the primary.

exercises.title: Benchmark query patterns
exercises.description: Compare a graph traversal, a document lookup, and a key-value read for the same data set.
exercises.expected_outcome: Each model should win on the access pattern it was built for.
